title: Year 1 New Zealand Curriculum - Ministry of Education
content:
## Year 1 New Zealand Curriculum
Understand what your child will have learned by the end of their first year of school. See suggestions for simple things you can do at home to help them learn.
- Guide for the New Zealand Curriculum – Years 0 to 8
- First 6 months at school
- Year 1 New Zealand Curriculum English in Year 1 Reading books at home in Year 1 Practising sounds and letters in Year 1 Mathematics and Statistics in Year 1 Health and Physical Education in Year 1 Science in Year 1 Technology in Year 1 Social Sciences in Year 1 The Arts in Year 1
- Reading books at home in Year 1
- Practising sounds and letters in Year 1
- Mathematics and Statistics in Year 1

We’re inviting parents and caregivers to share their feedback on the Parent Portal to help us improve the information and support we provide. Take a few minutes to complete our short survey.
Parent Portal survey – Survey Monkey

## By the end of Year 1 #
Knowing what your child is learning in their first full year of school will help you support and guide them as they begin learning reading, writing and maths every day.
- English in Year 1 For English in the first year at school, your child's teacher will focus on oral language, reading and writing. Find out more
For English in the first year at school, your child's teacher will focus on oral language, reading and writing.
- Reading books at home in Year 1 Find out about supporting your child’s reading in the first year of school. Find out more

### Reading books at home in Year 1
Find out about supporting your child’s reading in the first year of school.
- Practising sounds and letters in Year 1 Use these slides to help you practise sounds and letters (phonics) with your child. Find out more

### Practising sounds and letters in Year 1
Use these slides to help you practise sounds and letters (phonics) with your child.
- Mathematics and Statistics in Year 1 During the first 3 years at school, the goal is for children to thrive in environments rich in Maths. Find out more

### Mathematics and Statistics in Year 1
During the first 3 years at school, the goal is for children to thrive in environments rich in Maths.
- Health and Physical Education in Year 1 In their first year of school, your child will learn about growing bodies, health and hygiene, relationships, and foundational movement skills. Find out more

### Health and Physical Education in Year 1
In their first year of school, your child will learn about growing bodies, health and hygiene, relationships, and foundational movement skills.
- Science in Year 1 In Year 1, your child will begin to act like young scientists, exploring and asking questions about the world around them. Find out more
In Year 1, your child will begin to act like young scientists, exploring and asking questions about the world around them.
- Technology in Year 1 In Year 1, your child begins to be curious about design, turn ideas into simple creations, explore properties of materials, and develop creative thinking and problem-solving abilities. Find out more

### Technology in Year 1
In Year 1, your child begins to be curious about design, turn ideas into simple creations, explore properties of materials, and develop creative thinking and problem-solving abilities.
- Social Sciences in Year 1 In Year 1, your child will start to explore important concepts such as identity, family, and community, learning about family stories, places, and currency. Find out more

### Social Sciences in Year 1
In Year 1, your child will start to explore important concepts such as identity, family, and community, learning about family stories, places, and currency.
- The Arts in Year 1 In Year 1, your child will explore the Arts through simple concepts and techniques and engage with art forms from New Zealand and around the world. Find out more
In Year 1, your child will explore the Arts through simple concepts and techniques and engage with art forms from New Zealand and around the world.
